content.full_text:
January 15, 1982

Honorable Gordon Minder
Judge of the Superior Court
County of Alameda
1225 Fallon Street
Oakland, CA 94612

Dear Judge Minder:

This letter is in response to your letter dated January 4, 1982, in which you asked if it is necessary to list investments in money market funds on your Statement of Economic Interest.

Since money market funds are required to be registered as mutual funds with the Securities & Exchange Commission under the Investment Company Act of 1940, they need not be reported. (See Gov. Code Section 82030.)

Please call me at (16)322-6444 if you have any questions regarding this matter.

Yours truly,

Alice Hughes
Documents Examiner

AH:plh

January 4, 1982

Fair Political Practices Commission  
P.O. Box 807  
Sacramento, CA  

Gentlemen:

With reference to the information required by Statement of Economic Interests, Form 721, is it necessary to list investments in money market funds? If so, would it appear in Schedule A?

Very truly yours,

Gordon Minder  
Judge of the Superior Court
